hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6, thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is updated to emphasize the importance of disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to achieve higher selectivity for SSTR2.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions in off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations on these positions will help reduce off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets without affecting the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10) or Cys3/Cys14.
